name: Quito (Ecuador)

colors.symbolism: the blood of the Quiteños — the people of Quito
elements of the city's coat of arms

description: Six equally wide vertical stripes — the inner four red and the outer two blue — with the city's coat of arms (a fortified castle flanked by two black eagles holding spears, atop verdant rocks) charged in the centre.
history: Officially defined by Article 1 of Municipal Ordinance No. 1,634 passed in 1974 under Mayor Sixto Durán Ballén, with an earlier antecedent municipal decree from 1944. Pantone 185 C is specified for the red and Pantone 281 C for the blue.

features.symbolism: two blue outer stripes (Pantone 281 C) for the seas and the skies; four red inner stripes (Pantone 185 C) for the blood of the Quiteños

features.role: coat of arms of the Metropolitan District of Quito
features.symbolism: the city's coat of arms — a stone castle on verdant rocks flanked by two displayed black eagles (one bicephalous), evoking the Habsburg double-headed eagle and the title of Muy Noble y Muy Leal Ciudad de San Francisco de Quito granted by Charles V in 1541
